annotations.label: Control theory

anchor.snippet: is a field of control engineering and applied mathematics

annotations.note: Control theory as an engineering field is not a formalized mathematical object in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transfer function
annotations.note: No transfer-function notion exists in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: is a mathematical model of the relation between the input and output

annotations.label: Open-loop control

anchor.snippet: the control action from the controller is independent of the

annotations.note: Open-loop control is an engineering concept absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed-loop control

anchor.snippet: the control action from the controller is dependent on the process output

annotations.note: Closed-loop/feedback control is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed loop control system (BSI)

anchor.snippet: according to the British Standards Institution

annotations.note: This standards definition has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Feedback control system

anchor.snippet: A Feedback Control System is a system which tends to maintain a prescribed relationship

annotations.note: Feedback control systems are not modeled in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed-loop controller

anchor.snippet: A closed-loop controller or feedback controller is a control loop which incorporates feedback

annotations.note: No controller/feedback-loop construct exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Advantages of closed-loop control

anchor.snippet: Closed-loop controllers have the following advantages over open-loop controllers

annotations.note: These engineering claims are not stated or proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PID controller architecture

anchor.snippet: A common closed-loop controller architecture is the PID controller

annotations.note: PID controllers are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear control theory

anchor.snippet: This applies to systems made of devices which obey the superposition principle

annotations.note: Linearity exists via LinearMap, but linear control theory as such is not formalized.

annotations.label: Linear time invariant (LTI) systems

anchor.snippet: called linear time invariant (LTI) systems

annotations.note: LTI systems are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonlinear control theory

anchor.snippet: This covers a wider class of systems that do not obey the superposition principle

annotations.note: Nonlinear control theory is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frequency domain

anchor.section: Analysis techniques – frequency domain and time domain
anchor.snippet: the mathematical variables representing the system's input, output and feedback

annotations.note: Frequency-domain control analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Time-domain state space representation

anchor.section: Analysis techniques – frequency domain and time domain
anchor.snippet: the values of the state variables are represented as functions of time

annotations.note: Time-domain state-space models are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: State space representation

anchor.section: Analysis techniques – frequency domain and time domain
anchor.snippet: a mathematical model of a physical system as a set of input, output and state variables related by first-order differential equations

annotations.note: State-space representations of control systems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Single-input single-output (SISO)

anchor.snippet: This is the simplest and most common type, in which one output is controlled by one control signal

annotations.note: SISO system classification is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO)

anchor.snippet: These are found in more complicated systems

annotations.note: MIMO system classification is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lyapunov stability criteria

anchor.snippet: The stability of a general dynamical system with no input can be described with Lyapunov stability criteria

annotations.note: Grep for "Lyapunov" returns no Mathlib files, so Lyapunov stability is not formalized.

annotations.label: BIBO stability

anchor.snippet: A linear system is called bounded-input bounded-output (BIBO) stable if its output will stay bounded for any bounded input

annotations.note: BIBO stability has no Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Input-to-state stability (ISS)

anchor.snippet: Stability for nonlinear systems that take an input is input-to-state stability (ISS)

annotations.note: Input-to-state stability is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pole condition for stability

anchor.snippet: Mathematically, this means that for a causal linear system to be stable all of the poles of its transfer function must have negative-real values

annotations.note: The pole-location stability criterion is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotically stable

anchor.snippet: When the appropriate conditions above are satisfied a system is said to be asymptotically stable

annotations.note: Asymptotic stability of control systems is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Marginally stable

anchor.snippet: it is marginally stable

annotations.note: Marginal stability is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: BIBO stable pole inside unit circle

anchor.snippet: This system is BIBO (asymptotically) stable since the pole is inside the unit circle

annotations.note: This stability example relies on control concepts absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Not BIBO stable pole outside unit circle

anchor.snippet: and is not BIBO stable since the pole has a modulus strictly greater than one

annotations.note: This instability example relies on control concepts absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Controllability

anchor.snippet: Controllability is related to the possibility of forcing the system into a particular state by using an appropriate control signal

annotations.note: Controllability of state-space systems is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stabilizable

anchor.snippet: then the state is termed stabilizable

annotations.note: Stabilizability is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Observability

anchor.snippet: Observability instead is related to the possibility of observing

annotations.note: Observability of control systems is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Detectable

anchor.snippet: if a state cannot be observed it might still be detectable

annotations.note: Detectability is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robust controller

anchor.snippet: A robust controller is such that its properties do not change much if applied to a system slightly different

annotations.note: Robust controllers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: System identification

anchor.snippet: The process of determining the equations that govern the model's dynamics is called system identification

annotations.note: System identification is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decentralized control

anchor.snippet: the problem is one of decentralized control

annotations.note: Decentralized control is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic control problem

anchor.snippet: A stochastic control problem is one in which the evolution of the state variables is subjected to random shocks from outside the system

annotations.note: Stochastic control problems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deterministic control problem

anchor.snippet: A deterministic control problem is not subject to external random shocks

annotations.note: Deterministic control problems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Optimal control

anchor.snippet: Optimal control is a particular control technique in which the control signal optimizes a certain

annotations.note: Grep finds no optimal-control formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robust control

anchor.snippet: Robust control deals explicitly with uncertainty in its approach to controller design

annotations.note: Robust control is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic control

anchor.snippet: Stochastic control deals with control design with uncertainty in the model

annotations.note: Grep finds no stochastic-control formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adaptive control

anchor.snippet: Adaptive control uses on-line identification of the process parameters

annotations.note: Adaptive control is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hierarchical control system

anchor.snippet: A hierarchical control system is a type of control system in which a set of devices and governing software is arranged in a hierarchical tree

annotations.note: Hierarchical control systems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Intelligent control

anchor.snippet: Intelligent control uses various AI computing approaches

annotations.note: Intelligent control is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Self-organized criticality control

anchor.snippet: Self-organized criticality control may be defined as attempts to interfere in the processes

annotations.note: Self-organized criticality control is not formalized in Mathlib.
